raw_output: {"examples":[{"excerpt":"A slowdown or plateauing of patenting activity was observed in 2024 across most technology areas, though whether this represents a temporary fluctuation or a longer-term shift remains to be seen."},{"excerpt":"However, despite this overall expansion, 2024 saw the first slight decline in patent publications since 2014."},{"excerpt":"It is important to observe, however, that this growth trend peaked in 2021 with 5,884 publications (and in 2020 for IPFs); subsequent annual activity has decreased by roughly 13%."}]}

examples.excerpt: A slowdown or plateauing of patenting activity was observed in 2024 across most technology areas, though whether this represents a temporary fluctuation or a longer-term shift remains to be seen.
However, despite this overall expansion, 2024 saw the first slight decline in patent publications since 2014.
It is important to observe, however, that this growth trend peaked in 2021 with 5,884 publications (and in 2020 for IPFs); subsequent annual activity has decreased by roughly 13%.

raw_output: {"excerpt":"Not only does China have the largest share of all patent families published globally but also is growing at the rate of 50% per year. However, India has an even higher growth rate of 56% per year. The Republic of Korea has also achieved high growth rates in their GenAI patent families since 2014. By contrast, patent families from Japan and the UK have only risen by a little more than 10% per year."}

parsed_output.excerpt: Not only does China have the largest share of all patent families published globally but also is growing at the rate of 50% per year. However, India has an even higher growth rate of 56% per year. The Republic of Korea has also achieved high growth rates in their GenAI patent families since 2014. By contrast, patent families from Japan and the UK have only risen by a little more than 10% per year.
